fields.title: Hartman’s Nursing Assistant Care Vocabulary [2026/2027] Updated Version | Verified Terms & Detailed Rationales (GUARANTEED PASS)
fields.description: Hartman’s Nursing Assistant Care Vocabulary [2026/2027] Updated Version | Verified Terms & Detailed Rationales (GUARANTEED PASS)
fields.preview_text:
Hartman’s Nursing Assistant Care Vocabulary [2026/2027] 
Updated Version | Verified Terms & Detailed Rationales  
(GUARANTEED PASS)  
 
protected health information (PHI) - VERIFIED ANSWER - information that 
can be used to identify a person and relates to the patient's condition, any 
health care that the person has had, and payment for that health care; 
examples include a person's name, address, telephone number, social 
secur ity number, email address, and medical record number.  
 
electronic health records (EHR) - VERIFIED ANSWER - an electronic 
version of a patient's medical record that can be shared between 
healthcare organizations; includes information such as medical history , 
diagnoses, treatment plans, medications, immunization records, vital 
signs measurements, and imaging, test, and laboratory results.  
 
advance directives - VERIFIED ANSWER - legal documents that allow 
people to decide what medical care they wish to have in  the event they 
are unable to make those decisions themselves.  
 

living will - VERIFIED ANSWER - a document that outlines the medical care 
a person wants, or does not want, in case he becomes unable to make 
those decisions.  
 
durable power of attorney for he alth care - VERIFIED ANSWER - a signed, 
dated, and witnessed legal document that appoints someone else to make 
the medical decisions for a person in the event they become unable to do 
so. 
 
Physician Orders for Life -Sustaining Treatment (POLST) - VERIFIED 
ANSWER - a medical order that specifies the treatments a person wishes 
to receive, not what he wishes to avoid, when he is very ill; decisions are

the threat of punishment to force someone to act.  
 
How can Coercive Power be established? - VERIFIED ANSWER - By 
demonstrating a willingness to punish others, using emotional displays to 
intimidate, and lever aging organizational roles.  
 
What is an example of Coercive Power in a corporate context? - VERIFIED 
ANSWER - Ex-Volkswagen CEO Martin Winterkorn was feared by 
employees, contributing to the company's emissions scandal.  

 
What is Expert Power? - VERIFIED AN SWER - Expert Power is the capacity 
to influence others by possessing knowledge or skills they value.  
 
What is required for Expert Power to be effective? - VERIFIED ANSWER - 
There must be agreement that the power holder has the requisite 
knowledge.  
 
How ca n a manager establish Expert Power? - VERIFIED ANSWER - By 
demonstrating expertise, displaying credentials, and using relevant 
language.

capacity to influence others through identification with and respect for 
the power holder.  
 
How can Referent Power be established? - VERIFIED ANSWER - By 
developing relationships, creating community, and demonstrating admired 
characteristics like integrity and charisma.

A. 2 years  
B. 4 years  
C. 5 years  
D. 10 years  
  Answer: C

A. 16 years old  
B. 18 years old  
C. 21 years old  
D. 25 years old  
  Answer: B   
Explanation:  Minimum age requirement is 18 years .

fields.title: iHuman Case Study Evaluation and Management of a 16-YearOld Female Presenting with Acute Dysuria and Urinary Frequency (I’m peeing a lot and it hurts) in an Outpatient  Clinic Comprehensive Week #7, 2026
fields.description:
iHuman Case Study Evaluation and Management of a 16-YearOld Female Presenting with Acute Dysuria and Urinary Frequency (I’m peeing a lot and it hurts) in an Outpatient 
Clinic Comprehensive Week #7, 2026
fields.preview_text:
iHuman Case Study Evaluation and Management of a 16 -
YearOld Female  Presenting with Acute Dysuria and Urinary 
Frequency ( I’m peeing a lot and it hurts ) in an Outpatient   
Clinic  Comprehensive Week #7, 2026  
  
  
  
  


 
 
   
  
  
  
  
Chief Complaint & Opening Questions  
Q1. What brings you in today?   
A: “I’m peeing a lot and it hurts.”   
Q2. When did the symptoms begin?  
A: About 3 days ago.  
Q3. Have the symptoms been getting better, worse, or the 
same?   
A: They are getting worse.  
History of Present Illness (HPI)  
Q4. Describe the pain when you urinate.  
A: Burning pain when I pee.  
Q5. Do you feel an urgent need to urinate?  
A: Yes, I feel like I have to go all the time.  
Q6. Are you urinating small or normal amounts?   
A: Small amoun ts.  

 
 
 Q7. Any blood in your urine?  
A: No.  
Q8. Do you have suprapubic or lower abdominal pain?  
A: Yes, mild discomfort above the bladder.  
Q9. Any fever or chills?  
A: No.  
Q10. Any nausea or vomiting?  
A: No.  
  
Genitourinary & Gynecologic History  
Q11. Any vaginal discharge, itching, or odor?  
A: No.  
Q12. When was your last menstrual period?  
A: Two weeks ago.  
Q13. Are your periods regular?  
A: Yes.  
Q14. Are you sexually active?  
A: Yes.  
Q15. Do you use protection during sex?  
A: Sometimes condoms.  
Q16. Any new sexual partners?   
A: No, one partner.

A patient admitted to the ED with signs & symptoms of stroke. The stroke 
team should complete a comprehensive neurologic assessment and

cardiac arrest in a patient brought to the emergency department. Which 
finding on a 12 -lead ECG would confirm this suspicion? - VERIFIED ANSWER 
- Wide -complex ventricular rhythm or tall, pointed T waves  
 
A patient with dyspnea and a change  in mental status arrives at the 
emergency department. The healthcare team completes the necessary 
assessments and begins to care for the patient, including initiating 
cardiac monitoring, pulse oximetry, supplemental oxygen and vascular 
access. The team re views the patient's ECG rhythm strip, as shown in the

VERIFIED ANSWER - Atropine 0.5 mg every 4 to 5 minutes

fields.description: ACLS Post Test 1, 2, 3 & 4 Actual Questions with Verified Answers (2026 / 2027), 100% Guarantee Pass
fields.preview_text:
ACLS Post Test  1, 2, 3 & 4 Actual Questions with 
Verified Answers (2026 / 2027), 100% 
Guarantee Pass  
 
Question 1  
Which type of atrioventricular block best describes this rhythm?  
A) First -degree  
B) Second -degree type I  
C) Second -degree type II  
D) Third -degree  
VERIFIED ANSWER:  C) Second -degree type II  
Rationale:  Second -degree type II AV block is characterized by 
intermittent non -conducted P waves without PR interval prolongation 
before the dropped beat. This distinguishes it from type I (Wenckebach), 
which shows progressive PR l engthening.  
 
Question 2  
Your patient is in cardiac arrest and has been intuba ted. To assess CPR 
quality, which should you do?  

A) Monitor the patient's PETCO ₂ 
B) Obtain a 12 -lead ECG  
C) Check the patient's pulse  
D) Obtain a chest x -ray 
VERIFIED ANSWER:  A) Monitor the patient's PETCO ₂ 
Rationale:  PETCO₂ (end-tidal CO₂) correlates with cardiac output during 
CPR. A PETCO ₂ <10 mmHg suggests poor CPR quality; a sudden increase 
may indicate return of spontaneous circulation (ROSC).  
 
Question 3  
Which facility is the most appropriate EMS destination for a patient with 
sudden cardiac arrest who achieved return of spontaneous circul ation in 
the field?  
A) Comprehensive stroke care unit  
B) Acute rehabilitation care unit  
C) Acute long -term care unit  
D) Coronary reperfusion -capable medical center  
VERIFIED ANSWER:  D) Coronary reperfusion -capable medical center  
Rationale:  Post-cardiac arre st patients with ROSC should be transported 
to a facility capable of coronary angiography and PCI, as acute coronary 
occlusion is a common cause of sudden cardiac arrest.  
 

Question 4  
Which of the following signs is a likely indicator of cardiac arrest in an 
unresponsive patient?  
A) Slow, weak pulse rate  
B) Cyanosis  
C) Agonal gasps  
D) Irregular, weak pulse rate  
VERIFIED ANSWER:  C) Agonal gasps  
Rationale:  Agonal gasps are abnormal, sporadic, often irregular breaths 
that oc

Each authority is delegable unless specifically stated otherwise

A unique number identifying each solicitation, contract, agreement, or

A contract clause is a term or condition used in contr acts, or in both

The DFARS PGI is a companion resource to the DFARS

fields.title: SAPPC CERTIFICATION (SECURITY ASSET PROTECTION PROFESSIONAL CERTIFICATION) EXAM 2026/2027 QUESTIONS AND CORRECT DETAILED ANSWERS (GRADE A+)
fields.description: SAPPC CERTIFICATION (SECURITY ASSET PROTECTION PROFESSIONAL CERTIFICATION) EXAM 2026/2027 QUESTIONS AND CORRECT DETAILED ANSWERS (GRADE A+)
fields.preview_text:
SAPPC CERTIFICATION  (SECURITY ASSET PROTECTION PROFESSIONAL 
CERTIFICATION ) EXAM 2026/202 7 QUESTIONS AND CORRECT 
DETAILED ANSWERS (GRADE  A+) 
 
What is the difference between physical security surveys and physical 
security inspections? - VERIFIED ANSWER - A physical security survey is a 
formal, recorded assessment of an Installation's overall security 
program; physical security inspection is a f ormal, recorded compliance of 
physical procedures and measures implemented by a unit or activity to 
protect its assets.  
 
Who makes the determination of when physical security surveys and 
inspections are required? - VERIFIED ANSWER - • DoD Component 
Command ers. 
 
List three categories of Special Access Programs. - VERIFIED ANSWER - • 
Acquisition  
• Intelligence  
• Operations and Support  
 
Explain why access control measures are contingent on threat levels. - 
VERIFIED ANSWER - Based on threat levels, commanders o r directors are 

responsible for enhancing access control measures to their installations 
and facilities.  
 
Briefly define a Special Access Program. - VERIFIED ANSWER - A program 
established for a specific class of classified information that imposes 
safegua rding and access requirements that exceed those normally 
required for information at the same classification level.  
 
What is the definition of Critical Program Information in DoD? - VERIFIED 
ANSWER - U.S. capability elements that contribute to the warfight er's 
advantage throughout the life cycle, which, if compromised or subject to 
unauthorized disclosure, decrease the advantage.  
 
Elements or components of a Research, Development, and Acquisition 
(RDA) program that, if compromised, could cause significant d egradation 
in mission effectiveness; shorten the expected combat -effective life of the 
system; reduce technological advantage; significantly alter program 
direction; or enable an adversary to defeat, counter, copy, or reverse 
engineer the technology or cap ability.

fields.description: HESI Peds [2026/2027] Updated Version | Verified Pediatric Nursing Questions & Detailed Rationales (GRADED A+)
fields.preview_text:
HESI Peds [2026/2027] Updated Version | Verified 
Pediatric  Nursing Questions & Detailed Rationales  
(GRADED A+)  
 
A 3-week -old newborn is brought to the clinic for follow -up after a home 
birth. The mother reports that her child bo ttle feeds for 5 minutes only 
and then falls asleep. The nurse auscultates a loud murmur characteristic 
of a ventricular septal defect (VSD), and finds the newborn is acyanotic 
with a respiratory rate of 64 breaths per minute. What instruction should 
the n urse provide the mother to ensure the infant is receiving adequate 
intake? (Select all that apply.)  
A. Monitor the the infant's weight and number of wet diapers per day.  
B. Increase the infant's intake per feeding by 1 to 2 ounces per week.  
C. Mix the do se of prophylactic antibiotic in a full bottle of formula.  
D. Allow the infant to rest and refeed on demand or every 2 hours.  
E. Use a softer nipple or increase the size of the nipple opening. - VERIFIED 
ANSWER - A. Monitor the the infant's weight and numb er of wet diapers 
per day.  
B. Increase the infant's intake per feeding by 1 to 2 ounces per week.  
D. Allow the infant to rest and refeed on demand or every 2 hours.  
E. Use a softer nipple or increase the size of the nipple opening.  

 
A nurse caring for a ch ild with congestive heart failure provides 
instructions to the parents regarding the administration of digoxin 
(Lanoxin). Which statement by the mother indicates a need for further 
instructions?  
a. if my child vomits after I give the medication, I will not  repeat the dose  
b. I will check my child's pulse before giving the medication  
c. I will check the dose of the medication with my husband before I give 
the medication

medication w ith food

HESI ADHD [2026/2027] Updated Version | Verified 
Questions & Detailed Rationales  
 
After observing Jason exhibit behaviors that indicate his self -esteem may 
have suffered as a result of his ADHD, which interventions should the 
nurse implement to  improve his self esteem? - VERIFIED ANSWER - C. 
Encourage Jason to list three of his positive attributes  
D. Explore with Jason, the areas of his life that he wants to improve.  
E. Practice attentive listening when Jason is talking.  
 
Jason presents to the c linic for a follow up visit after a semester in 
college. He tells the nurse that he earned four B's and one A and that he is 
enjoying life at college.  
 
The nurse can measure improvement in Jason's social skills by which 
statement? - VERIFIED ANSWER - A. I have had the same girlfriend for 6 
months now  
 
When assessing Jason for improvement in his self esteem, what is the 
best approach for the nurse to use? - VERIFIED ANSWER - A. Tell me about 
the things that have improved this semester  

 
During the course of t he conversation, Jason tells the nurse that he has 
noticed some changes in the effectiveness of the medication. How should 
the nurse respond to Jason's comments? - VERIFIED ANSWER - B. Tell me 
about the changes in the effectiveness of your medications  
 
Jason tells the nurse that he sometimes has difficulty falling asleep. What 
is the first behavior the nurse should ask about when assessing Jason's 
sleep problem? - VERIFIED ANSWER - D. Medication schedule  
 
Jason asks the nurse if he has to continue to take ADHD medication for 
the rest of his life. How should the nurse respond to Jason's question? - 
VERIFIED ANSWER - C. Psychotherapy that focuses on ADHD may help 
eliminate medication.  
 
What should the nurse a ssess at Jason's follow up visit? - VERIFIED 
ANSWER - A. Nutritional status  
B. Mood/Anxiety disorders  
E. Coping mechanisms

The nurse is discussing dietary preferences with a client who adheres to 
a vegan diet. Which dietary su pplement should the nurse encourage the 
client to include in the dietary plan? - VERIFIED ANSWER - Vitamin B12.  
 
The nurse assesses an immobile, elderly male client and determines that 
his blood pressure is 138/60, his temperature is 95.8 F, and his output  is 
100 ml of concentrated urine during the last hour. He has wet -sounding 
lung sounds, and increased respiratory secretions. Based on these 
assessment findings, what nursing action is most important for the nurse 
to implement? - VERIFIED ANSWER - Turn the  client q2h  
 
The nurse is giving pre -op instructions to a 14 yr old female scheduled for 
surgery to correct a spinal curvature. Which stmt best demonstrates that 
leaning has taken place? - VERIFIED ANSWER - "I understand that I will be 
in a body cast and I will show you how you taught me to turn"  
 

To take the v/s of a 4 month old, which ordered will give the most 
accurate results? - VERIFIED ANSWER - RR - HR - rectal temp  
 
During routine screening at a school clinic, an otoscope exam reveal s a 
tympanic mb that is pearly gray, slightly bulging, and not movable. What 
action should the nurse take next? - VERIFIED ANSWER - Ask the child if 
they have had a cold, runny nose, or any ear pain lately  
 
Which restraint should be used for a toddler afte r a cleft palate repair? - 
VERIFIED ANSWER - Elbow  
 
What pre -op nsg intervention should be included in the POC for an infant 
w/ pyloric stenosis? - VERIFIED ANSWER - Observe for projectile vomiting  
 
(remember that projectile vomiting leads to metabolic alk alosis)  
 
A 6 month old returns from surgery w/ elbow restraints in place. What 
nsg care should be included when caring for any restrained child? - 
VERIFIED ANSWER - Remove the restraints one at a time

constitution, calling for proportional representation in both houses of a 
bicameral Congress. The plan favored larger states and thus prompted 
smaller states to come back with their own plan for apportioning 
representat ion.  
 
McCulloch v. Maryland - ✔✔ ANSW✔✔ ..Maryland was trying to tax the 
national bank and Supreme Court ruled that federal law was stronger 
than the state law  
  
Plessy v. Ferguson - ✔✔ ANSW✔✔ ..a 1896 Supreme Court decision 
which legalized state ordered segregation so long as the facilities for 
blacks and whites were equal  
  
Brown v. Board of Education - ✔✔ ANSW✔✔ ..1954 - The Supreme  
Court overruled Plessy v. Ferguson, declared that racially segregated  
facilities are inherently unequal and ordered all public schools    
desegregated.    
  
Washington v. Davis - ✔✔ ANSW✔✔ ..Court ruling that dealt with job  
testing and discrimination. DC Cops    
  
Marbury v. Madison - ✔✔ ANSW✔✔ ..This case establishes the  

Supreme Court's power of Judicial Review    
  
Executive Branch checks - ✔✔ ANSW✔✔ ..Veto law on legislative,  
pardon criminals from judicial.    
  
Judicial Branch checks - ✔✔ ANSW✔✔ ..On Executive  
-can declare acts unconstitutional  
-serve for life  
On Legislative  
-can declare acts unconstitutional  
-serve for life

fields.title: iHuman Case Study Comprehensive (Week 7, Clinical Assessment and Management of Acute Dysuria and Urinary Frequency( "I’m peeing a lot and it hurts") in a 16-Year-Old Female
fields.description: iHuman Case Study Comprehensive (Week 7, Clinical Assessment and Management of Acute Dysuria and Urinary Frequency( "I’m peeing a lot and it hurts") in a 16-Year-Old Female
fields.preview_text:
iHuman Case Study Comprehensive (Week  7, 
Clinical Assessment and  Management of Acute 
Dysuria and Urinary  Frequency(  "I’m peeing a 
lot and it hurts ") in  a 16 -Year -Old Female   
 
 
  
   
Clinical Assessment and Management of Acute Dysuria and Urinary 
Frequency in a 16 -Year -Old Female  


 
 
 Chief Complaint: “I’m peeing a lot and it hurts.”   
  
Patient Profile  
• Age: 16 years  
• Sex: Female  
• Setting: Outpatient clinic (with laboratory capabilities)  
• Source of History: Patient (reliable)  
• Accompanied by: Mother (present in waiting area; consent 
obtained per adolescent confidentiality guidelines)  
  
History of Present Illness (HPI)  
The patient is a 16 -year -old female who presents with acute onset 
dysuria and urinary frequency for the past 3 days. She reports a 
burning sensation during urination, increased urgency, and voiding 
small amounts frequently. Symptoms began suddenly and have 
progressively worsened.  
She denies flank pain, fever, chills, nausea, vomiting, or vaginal 
discharge. She reports suprapubic discomfort, rated 4/10, 
nonradiating. No hematuria noted visually. She has not tried 
antibiotics but has increased fluid intake with minimal relief.  
She reports recent sexual activity with one male partner and 
inconsistent condom use. No prior history of urinary tract infections. 
Last menstrual period was 2 weeks ago, regular cycles.  
  
HPI Question & Answer Highlights (i -Human Style)  
 Question  Patient Response  

 
 
 When did symptoms start? 3 days ago  
Pain with urination?  Yes, burning  
Frequency/urgency?  Yes, very frequent  
Fever or chills?  No  
Flank pain?  No  
Vaginal discharge/itching? No  
Sexual activity?  Yes  
Contraception use?  Sometimes condoms  
Prior UTIs?  No  
Pregnancy risk?  Possibly  
  
Review of Systems (ROS)  
General: No fever, chills, weight loss  
GU: Dysuria, frequency, urgency, suprapubic pain; no flank pain  
GI: No nausea, vomiting, diarrhea  
GYN: No discharge, itching

Framework, An Introduction  New 2026 Update  
Questions and  Verified Answers  Grade A  100%  Correct   
 
 
  
 
florida  
  
 A MAC Group is:    
  
A. FEMA's focal point for national resource coordination.   
B. The central location from which off -scene activities supported by the 
state or tribal government are coordinated.   
C. Composed of senior officials, such as agency administrators, 
executives, or their designees, who are authorized to represent or 
commit agency resources and funds in support of incident activities.   
D. A coordinatio n center that expands to become an interagency facility 
in anticipation of a serious incident or immediately following an 
incident. - ,,,,answer,,,, ..C. Composed of senior officials, such as 
agency administrators, executives, or their designees, who are 
authorized to represent or commit agency resources and funds in 
support of incident activities.  
  

The Unified Coordination Group:    
  
A. Is a temporary Federal facility.   
B. Directs the tactics employed at the incident site.   
C. Provides coordination leadership at the Joint Field Office.   
D. Is a state -level group comprised of the heads of each Emergency 
Support Function (ESF). - ,,,,answer,,,, ..C. Provides coordination 
leadership at the Joint Field Office.  
  
What is the correct fu nction for the National Center for Missing & 
Exploited Children (NCMEC)?   
A. Provides volunteers with the proper training for emergency 
preparedness B. Facilitates the identification of children and their 
reunification   
C. Shares knowledge and resources  throughout the disaster cycle  D. 
Provides relief to survivors and help people prevent, prepare, and 
respond to emergencies - ,,,,answer,,,, ..B. Facilitates the identification of 
children and their reunification  
 
Stabilizing community lifelines is the primary effort during?   
  
A. Recovery   
B. Pre-Incident   
C. Post Incident   
D. Incident Response - ,,

A+
fields.preview_text:
FLORIDA LAWS AND RULES (DENTAL  EXAM ADEX CDCA) EXAM 
STUDY GUIDE  UPDATED VERSION ALREADY GRADED   
A+  
 
 
 florida  
  
1. The primary purpose of the Florida Board of Dentistry is to:  
A. Promote dental businesses  
B. Protect public health and safety  
C. Increase dental school enrollment  
D. Set dental fees  
Answer:  B  
  
2. The Florida Board of Dentistry is governed under which 
statute?  
A. Chapter 456, FS  
B. Chapter 468, FS  
C. Chapter 466, FS  

 
D. Chapter 400, FS  
Answer:  C  
  
3. How many total members serve on the Florida Board of 
Dentistry?  
A. 9  
B. 10  
C. 11  
D. 12  
Answer:  C  
  
4. Which group makes up the majority of the Florida Board of 
Dentistry?  
A. Consumer members  
B. Dental hygienists  
C. Dentists  
D. Dental assistants  
Answer:  C  

 
  
5. A dental hygienist in Florida may perform scaling and root 
planing under:  
A. Direct supervision only  
B. Indirect supervision only  
C. General supervision only  
D. Dentist supervision as permitted by law  
Answer:  D  
  
6. Under direct supervision, the dentist must:  
A. Be on the premises only  
B. Be available by phone  
C. Examine the patient before and after the procedure  
D. Authorize treatment in writing  
Answer:  C  
  
7. Under general supervision, the dentist:  
A. Must be physically present

Mindfulness - ,,,,answer,,,, ..state of being attentive,  careful, 
observant,  
always paying attention to details when giving 
meds    
  
Pharmacist - ,,,,answer,,,, ..person skilled in preparing meds and 
having  

the license to do so, a 
druggist

prescribe medications and treat the people you support.

give as  
needed    
  
standarization - ,,,,answer,,,, ..process of always following the same 
steps  
in administering

Two rights of the people you are giving medication to 
are... -   

,,,,answer,,,, ..1. Right to be free from too much 
medication

risks/benefits  
  
Three principles to keep in mind when giving meds 
are... -   
,,,,answer,,,, ..Mindfulness, maximizing capabilities, and

changes

fields.title: Manage First Controlling Foodservice Costs Final Exam Review Questions and Answers 2026 (GUARANTEED PASS)
fields.description: Manage First Controlling Foodservice Costs Final Exam Review Questions and Answers 2026 (GUARANTEED PASS)
fields.preview_text:
Manage First Controlling Foodservice Costs Final 
Exam Review Questions and Answers  2026  
(GUARANTEED PASS ) 
 
How do managers measure the overall average food cost percentage 
when the menu includes some items that sell at lower or high er food cost 
percentages? - VERIFIED ANSWER - -They measure by looking carefully at 
the menu product mix. The menu product mix is a detailed analysis that 
shows the quantities sold of each menu item, along with their selling 
prices and standard portion cos ts. 
-A sample menu mix percentage (MM%) might look like this:  
# of pork chops sold / total entrees sold = menu mix %  
-The menu product mix is used to determine the composite (potential) 
food cost percentage  
 
What is accounts payable? - VERIFIED ANSWER - -Accounts payable 
represents the amount of money that an operati on owes suppliers from 
which it has purchased goods, products, or services  
-The first step of the accounts payable process begins when an invoice is 
brought to the manager's office.  

-Next, the manager needs to make sure that the invoice is addressed to 
the correct organization.  
-The manager needs to make sure that the invoice is signed by one of the 
operation's employees, indicating that item amounts are correct and 
contents are intact.  
-If everything is correct, the manager should stamp the invoice 
"Appro ved," which signifies that it should be paid. After the invoice is 
approved it needs to be coded. That is, if an invoice contains flour and 
vodka, the flour should be coded under food costs, and vodka should be 
coded under the beverage costs category.  
 
The manager should oversee accounts payable activities with these goals 
in mind: - VERIFIED ANSWER - • Pay invoices on a predetermined schedule 
established by the business, not the vendors.  
• Make sure all invoices that are paid are accurate and authentic.  
• Minimize the amount of time and expense in processing accounts 
payable paperwork.  
 
AP Cost - VERIFIED ANSWER - -Me

fields.description: Prepare for your Medical Surgical IEN comprehensive review with key topics like complex care, pharmacology, and health assessment. Plus notable schools offering CNO-approved IEN pathways (2026)
fields.preview_text:
Prepare for your Medical Surgical IE N 
comprehensive review with key topics like 
complex care, pharmacology, and health 
assessment. Plus notable schools offering 
CNO -approved IEN pathways (2026)  
 
 
YORK UNI  
1. A patient with chest pain relieved by rest most likely has:  
A. MI  
B. Stable angina    
C. Unstable angina  
D. Pericarditis  
Rationale:  Stable angina is predictable and relieved by rest or 
nitroglycerin.  
2. First priority for suspected MI?  
A. Pain medication  
B. Oxygen  
C. ECG    
D. Nitroglycerin  
Rationale:  Rapid ECG confirms ischemia and guides treatment.  
3. Which lab confirms MI?  
A. CK  
B. BNP  

C. Troponin I    
D. D-dimer  
Rationale:  Troponin is cardiac -specific and most sensitive.  
4. Left-sided heart failure causes:  
A. Peripheral edema  
B. Ascites  
C. Pulmonary crackles    
D. JVD  
Rationale:  Left HF → pulmonary congestion.   
5. Digoxin toxicity sign?  
A. Hypertension  
B. Yellow -green halos    
C. Bradycardia only  
D. Hyperkalemia  
Rationale:  Visual disturbances are classic.  
6. Beta blockers are contraindicated in:  
A. HTN  
B. HF C. Asthma    
D. CAD  
Rationale:  Can cause bronchoconstriction.  
7. BP 86/50 post -op—priority action?  
A. Recheck later  
B. Notify provider C. Increase IV  fluids    
D. Give antihypertensive  
Rationale:  Hypotension → volume support first.   
8. Most common cause of HF exacerbation?  
A. Infection  
B. Medication nonadherence    
C. MI  

D. Valvular disease  
9. Best position for acute pulmonary edema?  
A. Supine  
B. Trendelenburg C. High Fowler’s   D. Side -
lying  
10. Heparin antidote?  
A. Vitamin K B. 
Protamine sulfate    
C. Naloxone  
D. Calcium gluconate  
11. INR goal for warfarin therapy?  
A. 0.8–1.2  
B. 1.0–1.5 C. 2.0 –3.0    
D. >4.0  
12. First sign of hypovolemic shock?  
A. Hypotension  
B. Oliguria C. Tachycardia    
D. Cyanosis  
13. Best diet for HTN?  
A. Keto  
B. DASH    
C. High protein  
D. High sodium  
14. Cardiac tamponade sign?  
A

fields.description: TAP Series: Food Safety Training 2026 Exam Questions and Verified Answers 100% Pass (GRADED A+)
fields.preview_text:
TAP Series: Food Safety Training 2026 Exam 
Questions and  Verified  Answers 100% Pass  
(GRADED A+)  
 
A chef sanitized a thermometer probe and then checked the temperature 
of beef st ew being held in a hot holding unit. The temperature was 120F, 
which did not meet the operation's critical limit of 135F. The chef recorded 
the temperature of the stew in the log and reheated the stew to 165F for 
15 seconds. Which was the corrective action ? - VERIFIED ANSWER - 
Reheating the stew  
 
An operation received a violation in the outside area of the facility. The 
manager reviewed the area and saw that the dumpster was placed on 
gravel. The lids were closed, and the drain plug was in place to prevent 
the dumpster from draining. What is the problem. - VERIFIED ANSWER - 
The surface underneath the dumpster should have been paved with 
concrete or asphalt  
 
A father takes his four -year-old daughter and her friend to a local 
hamburger diner after swimming lessons. The friend wants a hot dog, and 
his daughter  wants a hamburger. Because the father likes his 
hamburgers rare, he orders a rare hamburger for himself and his 

daughter. What should the server do? - VERIFIED ANSWER - Explain that 
the restaurant cannot serve rare hamburgers to young children  
 
 
 
A woman runs out of the bathroom in a fast food restaurant, and 
frantically tells the manager that  the toilet has backed up and is over 
flowing into the service area. What should the manager do? - VERIFIED 
ANSWER - Close the restaurant and report the incident to the local health 
department  
 
A food handler pulled a hotel pan of egg salad from the cooler and used it 
to prepare four egg salad sandwiches, What is the problem with this 
situation? - VERIFIED ANSWER - Time temperature abuse  
 
A food handler prepares and delivers meals to elderly individual s 
receiving services at home. What symptoms require this food handler to 
stay home from work? - VERIFIED ANSWER - Sore throat with fever  
 
A food

fields.title: WGU C207 Data-Driven Decision Making  Objective Assessment Exam 2026 –  Verified Questions with Correct Answers  (100% Guaranteed Pass
fields.description:
WGU C207 Data-Driven Decision Making 
Objective Assessment Exam 2026 – 
Verified Questions with Correct Answers 
(100% Guaranteed Pass
fields.preview_text:
WGU C207 Data -Driven Decision Making   
Objective Assessment Exam 2026 –  
Verified Questions with Correct Answers   
(100% Guaranteed Pass   
 
 
  
 In a normal distribution, approximately what percentage of data 
falls within ±1 standard deviation  of the mean?  
A. 50%   
B. 68%     
C. 95%   
D. 99.7%   
Rationale:   
This comes from the Empirical Rule (68 –95–99.7) , a frequent 
WGU test topic.  
  
The Central Limit Theorem states that as sample size increases:  

 
 
 A. The population becomes normal   
B. The sample mean distribution becomes normal     
C. Variance increases   
D. Bias increases   
Rationale:   
CLT applies to the sampling distribution of the mean , not the 
population itself  
Which statistic best summarizes the center of a skewed income 
distribution?  
A. Mean  B. 
Median     
C. Standard deviation   
D. Variance   
Rationale:   
Income data are typically right -skewed due to extreme high values. 
The median  is resistant to outliers, while the mean is pulled toward 
extreme values. WGU frequently tests this concept.  
  
A researcher surveys 300 customers from a company with 50,000 
customers. The 300 customers represent:  

 
 
 A. A population  
B. A census   
C. A sample     
D. A parameter   
Rationale:   
A sample  is a subset of a population. Parameters describe 
populations; statistics descr ibe samples.  
  
If the probability of an event occurring is 0.20, what is the 
probability it does NOT occur?  
A. 0.20   
B. 0.40   
C. 0.80     
D. 1.20  
Rationale:   
Probabilities must sum to 1.  
1 − 0.20 = 0.80   
  
.

Decreased neuromuscular excitability is often the result of a.

a. hypokalemia  
b. hyperkalemia  
c. hypocalcemia  
d. hypercalcemia - ✔✔ANSW✔✔..A  
  
Abnormalities in intracellular regulation of enzyme activity and cellular  
production of ATP are as sociated with    
a. hyponatremia    
  b. hypocalcemia  
c. hypophosphatemia    
d. hypokalemia - ✔✔ANSW✔✔..C   
  
The fraction of total body water (TBW) volume contained in the intracellular 
space in adults is a. three fourths  
b. two thirds  
c. one half  
d. one third - ✔✔ANSW✔✔..B  
  
Clinical manifestations of severe symptomatic hypophosphatemia are caused by

space in adults is a. three fourths  
b. two thirds  
c. one half  
d. one third - ✔✔ANSW✔✔..B  
  
Clinical manifestations of severe symptomatic hypophosphatemia are caused by  
a. excess proteins

c. deficiency of ATP    
d. hypocalcemia - ✔✔ANSW✔✔..C

fields.description: NR 547 Telepsychiatry Practice Examination: 50 Graduate-Level Questions With Verified Answers & Rationales for PMHNP Students (2026)
fields.preview_text:
NR 547 Telepsychiatry Practice 
Examination: 50 Graduate -Level 
Questions With Verified Answers & 
Rationales for PMHNP Students (2026)  
 
 
 
 DEVRY UNI  
  
Definition  
Telepsychiatry  is the delivery of psychiatric assessment, diagnosis, 
treatment, and follow -up care using telecommunication technologies , 
most commonly secure video conferencing. It is a subspecialty of 
telemedicine and is widely used in mental health care to improve acce ss 
and continuity of services.  
  
Scope of Telepsychiatry Practice  
Telepsychiatry allows psychiatric providers, including PMHNPs, to 
deliver:  
• Psychiatric evaluations  
• Medication management  

 
 
 • Individual psychotherapy  
• Family therapy  
• Crisis intervention  
• Follow -up and monitoring of chronic mental illness  
• Patient education and counseling  
  
Clinical Indications  
Telepsychiatry is appropriate for patients with:  
• Depression and anxiety disorders  
• Bipolar disorder (stable patients)  
• ADHD  
• PTSD  
• Schizophrenia  (stable, non -acute patients)  
• Substance use disorders (non -emergent situations)  
  Not appropriate for :  
• Psychiatric emergencies (active suicidal or homicidal risk)  
• Patients requiring immediate inpatient care  
• Situations requiring physical restraint or emergency medication  
  
Benefits of Telepsychiatry  
1. Improved Access to Care  o Reaches rural 
and underserved populations o Reduces 
wait times for psychiatric services  
2. Continuity of Care  o Improves follow -up 
adherence o Reduces missed appointments  

 
 
 3. Patient Satisfaction  o Increased comfort 
in familiar environments o Reduced 
stigma of visiting mental health clinics  
4. Cost -Effectiveness  o Decreased 
transportation costs o Efficient use of 
provider time  
  
Limit ations and Challenges  
• Limited ability to perform physical examinations  
• Technology barriers (internet access, digital literacy)  
• Privacy concerns if patient lacks a confidential space  
• Difficulty a

Parent/caregiver reports concern about the child’s growth and/or

The patient is a pediatric client presenting for a developmental 
evaluation due to concerns regarding delayed growth and/or 
developmental milestones . The caregiver reports that the child has not 
met age -appropriate expectations in one or more developmental domains 
(gross motor, fine motor, language, social, or cognitive). No acute 
illness is reported. Appe tite, sleep patterns, and elimination habits are 
reviewed, with attention to any contributing nutritional or environmental 
factors.  
  
Past Medical History  
• Prenatal/birth history: (e.g., full -term, no complications OR history 
of prematurity if applicable )  
• Immunizations: Up to date per CDC schedule  
• Chronic illnesses: Denied unless otherwise noted  
• Hospitalizations/surgeries: None reported

fields.preview_text:
WGU D358 Global HRM Comprehensive  Study Guide with Answers  
Questions and Answers  2026 Updates  
  
  
 Which staffing approach emphasizes hiring the best talent 
regardless of nationality?  
A. Ethnocentric  
B. Polycentric  
C. Regiocentric  
D. Geocentric  
  Answer: D   
Rationale:   
Geocentric strategy  focuses on global talent optimization and is common 
in multinational corporations with integrated global operations.  
  
What is a key disadvantage of using PCNs for international 
assignments?  
A. Lack of company knowledge  
B. High cost and adjustment challen ges  
C. Limited technical skills  
D. Poor communication ability  

  Answer: B   
Rationale:   
PCNs often involve high relocation costs , tax equalization, and risk of 
culture shock , which can lead to assignment failure.  
  
Which Hofstede dimension measures acceptance of unequal power 
distribution?  
A. Individualism  
B. Masculinity  
C. Power Distance  
D. Uncertainty Avoidance  
  Answer: C   
Rationale:   
Power Distance  reflects how comfortable a culture is with hierarchy and 
authority differences —critical for leadership and HR policy decisions.  
 
1. What is the primary difference between domestic HRM and global 
HRM?  
A. Global HRM focuses only on recruitment  
B. Global HRM manages employees across different legal and cultural 
systems  

C. Domestic HRM is more strategic  
D. Domestic HRM involves more compliance  
  Answer: B   
Rationale:   
Global HRM must operate across multiple countries , each with different 
labor laws, cul tures, currencies, and ethical norms . This complexity is 
what distinguishes it from domestic HRM.  
  
2. Which factor most strongly drives organizations to globalize their 
workforce?  
A. Employee satisfaction  
B. Unionization  
C. Market expansion opportunities  
D. Reduced training needs  
  Answer: C   
Rationale:   
Companies globalize primarily to enter new markets , increase revenue, 
and remain competitive. HR must then support staffing a

COSMETOLOGY HAWAII LICENSE EXAM 3  VERSIONS  
 
 
 
Which item must be discarded after each client?  
A. Metal pusher  
B. Electric clipper  
C. Emery board  
D. Shears  
  Correct Answer: C. Emery board   
Rationale:   
Emery boards are porous  and cannot be properly disinfected. 
Hawaii and NIC rules require all porous items to be single -use and 
discarded  after each client.  
  
5. Hair, skin, and nails are primarily composed of:  
A. Collagen  
B. Elastin  

  
 
   
  C. Keratin  
D. Melanin  
Correct Answer: C. Keratin   

  
Rationale:   
 
   
  Keratin is a fibrous protein  that form s the structure of hair, skin, 
and nails. Understanding keratin is essential for chemical services 
like coloring and relaxing   
1. Removing visible dirt, debris, and some germs from tools 
and surfaces is called:  
A. Sterilization  
B. Sanitation  
C. Disinfection  
D. Antisepsis  
  Correct Answer: B. Sanitation   
Rationale:   
Sanitation is the lowest level of decontamination . It removes 
visible dirt and reduces germs to a safe level but does not kill all 
bacteria or viruses . This step is required before disinfection 
because disinfectants cannot work properly on dirty surfaces.

RESNA ATP Practice Exam Questions and Verified Answers – 
Latest  Version 2025/2026 – 100% Pass   
 
 
  
 When completing a mobility device evaluation, whi ch factor should 
be addressed FIRST?  
A. Funding source  
B. Client preference  
C. Medical and functional needs  
D. Aesthetics  
  Correct Answer: C   
RATIONALE:   
ATP exams prioritize medical necessity and function  first. Funding and 
preference come later, after clinical justification is established.  
  
5. A client demonstrates extensor tone when seated. Which 
intervention is MOST appropriate?  
A. Increase seat -to-floor height  
B. Add posterior pelvic support  
C. Increase se at depth  
D. Remove trunk supports  

  Correct Answer: B   
RATIONALE:   
Posterior pelvic support helps reduce extensor tone  by stabilizing the 
pelvis, which improves overall posture and function.  
  
6. What is the PRIMARY purpose of a pelvic positioning belt?  
A. Restraint  
B. Pressure relief  
C. Postural stability  
D. Fall prevention only  
  Correct Answer: C   
RATIONALE:   
Pelvic belts are positioning devices , not restraints. Their purpose is to 
stabilize the pelvis , which is the foundation for all seated posture.  
 
1. The PRIMARY role of the ATP in wheelchair provision is to:  
A. Recommend the least expensive device  
B. Prescribe medical treatment  
C. Match the technology to the user’s functional needs   
D. Train caregivers only  

  Correct Answer: C   
RATIONALE:   
The ATP’s role is functional matching  — analyzing the user’s physical 
abilities, environment, and goals  to recommend appropriate assistive 
technology. Cost and training matter, but they are secondary  to 
functional outcomes.  
  
2. A client with a C6 spinal cord injury needs independent pressure 
relief. What is the BEST seating solution?  
A. Manual wheelchair wi th foam cushion  
B. Power tilt -in-space wheelchair  
C. Reclining wheelchair  
D. Scooter  
  Correct Answer: B   
RATIONALE:   
Clients with limited trunk control an

to participate in covalent enzyme catalysis - VERIFIED ANSWER - Leu 
 
Which statement is correct concerning the T(deoxy) and R(oxy) states of 
tetrameric hemoglobin - VERIFIED ANSWER - In the T to R transition, the 
central cavity of the tetramer decreases in size, preventing the binding of

and serves as a positive allosteric effector for - VERIFIED ANSWER -

substrate concentration

In a patient with pseudohypertrophic Duchenne muscular dystrophy, 
which of the following is NOT a typical sleep -related problem? - VERIFIED

b. Decrease in vital capacity to less than 2 L  
c. Central sleep apnea  
d. Increasing sleep disruption  
 
In patients with severe sleep -disordered breathing, the most commonly 
seen response resulting from successful PAP titration is:  
a. Increased hypoxemia  
b. Attenuation of alpha activity  
c. REM and slow wave rebound

desensitization  
 
The most appropriate action a technologist would take when there is 
confirmed ventricular tachycardia lasting greater that 30 seconds is:  
a. Initiate emergency response system  
b. Assess, document and continue to mon itor 
c. No immediate response is needed  
d. Discontinue recording and transport to emergency room - VERIFIED 
ANSWER - Initiate emergency response system  
 
Which of the following is characteristic of sleep during the second 
trimester of pregnancy? - VERIFIED ANSWER - a. Total sleep time (TST) 
decreases to pre pregnancy levels.  
b. Slow -wave sleep (SWS) decreases.  
c. REM sleep decreases.  

d. Shortness of breath decreases.

VERIFIED ANSWER - a. cardiac output  
b. pulmonary vascular resistance  
c. stroke volume  
d. cardiac index

during stage W, NREM, and REM sleep are known as: - VERIFIED ANSWER -

fields.description:

CORE (SUPERVISOR) EXAM STUDY GUIDE Verified 
Answers with Rationales NEWEST 2026 Edition 
(Guaranteed Pass)
fields.preview_text:
CONNECTICUT COMMERCIAL PESTICIDE APPLICATOR 
CORE (SUPERVISOR) EXAM STUDY GUIDE  Verified 
Answers with Rationales NEWEST 2026 Edition 
(Guaranteed Pass ) 
 
Question 1  
A pesticide label is a legal document that must be followed.  
A) True  
B) True  
C) False  
D) Optional guidance  
VERIFIED ANSWER:  A) True  
Rationale:  The pesticide label is a legal document under both federal and 
state law  and must be followed.  
 
Question 2  
The active ingredient in a pesticide prod uct is defined as the chemical 
that: 
A) Enhances storage stability  
B) Makes the product smell better  

C) Controls the target pest  
D) Adds color  
VERIFIED ANSWER:  C) Controls the target pest  
Rationale:  Active ingredients are the chemicals that provide pestici dal 
activity against the target pest.  
 
Question 3  
The signal word "Danger" on  a pesticide label indicates:  
A) Low toxicity  
B) High toxicity  
C) No toxicity  
D) Only toxicity to plants  
VERIFIED ANSWER:  B) High toxicity  
Rationale:  Signal words reflect acute toxicity; "Danger" indicates the 
highest hazard category (Category I).  
 
Question 4  
PPE stands for:  
A) Personal protective education  
B) Personal protective equipment  

C) Pesticide processing equipment  
D) Professional pract ice evaluation  
VERIFIED ANSWER:  B) Personal protective equipment  
Rationale:  PPE means equipment worn to reduce exposure to pesticides.  
 
Question 5  
The four routes of pesticide exposure include all except:  
A) Inhalation  
B) Dermal  
C) Ingestion  
D) Radiation  
VERIFIED ANSWER:  D) Radiation  
Rationale:  Radiation is not a route of pesticid e exposure; the four main 
routes are dermal, inhalation, ingestion, and ocular.  
 
Question 6  
The primary factor that determines how long a pesticide persists in the 
environment is:  
A) Time of year  
B) Chemical properties

ACTION Personal Trainer exam Questions and Answers 
with complete solution  (GUARANTEED PASS ) 
 
1. When using correct skinfold measurements to evaluate body fat, the 
standard estimation of error is approximately ________% when an 
experienced tester performs the assessment.  
a. 1-2% 
b. 3-4% 
c. 5-6% 
d. Skinfold assessment value does not have a standard error - VERIFIED 
ANSWER - b. 3-4% 
 
2. Bronchodilators are sometimes used as medication for asthmatics. If 
during the screening it is identified that a person routinely takes these 
medications, how will the medication influence exer cise prescription?  
a. It will have little or no effect  
b. It will require decreased heart r ate intensities during exercise  
c. It w ill require longer rest periods  
d. It will require lower intensities applied for longer duration - VERIFIED 
ANSWER - a. It will have little or no effect  

 
3. Which of the following body composition scores for a female would 
indicate morbid obesity and the need for medical clearance prior to 
exercise participation?  
a. >40%  
b. >35%  
c. >32%  
d. Body fat percentage is not a criteria indicative of medical clearance - 
VERIFIED ANSWER - a. >40%  
 
4. Which of the following is NOT a possible cause of increased estimation 
error when performing exe rcise tests for muscular fitness?  
a. Poor explanation of the test procedures and perfor mance criteria used 
for scoring  
b. Lack of client profi ciency wi th the tested movement  
c. Loose sc oring guidelines by the trainer  
d. Practicing the test activities t o ensure technique proficiency - VERIFIED 
ANSWER - d. Practicing the test activities to ensure technique proficiency  
 

5. Which of the following forms provides the most information for making 
an informed decision regarding physical activity participation wh en 
screening clients for exercise?  
a. Par -Q 
b. Health Status Questionnaire  
c. Dietary Log  
d. Informed Consent - VERIFIED ANSWER - b. Health Status Questionnaire

Tennessee Commercial / Industrial Contractor Exam (BC -B,C) Exam 
Practice Questions And Correct Answers (Verified Answers) Plus 
Rationale 2026  Instant Download Pdf  (GRUARANTEED PASS ) 
 
Question 1  
A contractor bidding on a Tennessee commercial/industrial project must 
ensure compliance with licensin g requirements. Which of the following 
best describes the limitation of a BC -B,C license classification?  
A) It allows unrestricted residential construction only  
B) It limits work strictly to electrical installations  
C) It permits both commercial and indust rial construction within the 
monetary limits of the license  
D) It allows only subcontracting work under a general contractor  
VERIFIED ANSWER: C) It permits both commercial and industrial 
construction within the monetary limits of the license  
Rationale: The  BC-B,C classification authorizes contractors to perform 
both commercial and industrial construction projects, provided they 
remain within their approved monetary limit as set by the Tennessee 
Board for Licensing Contractors.  
 
Question 2  
In Tennessee, wha t is the primary purpose of requiring contractors to 
maintain a monetary limit on their license?  

A) To determine tax liability  
B) To ensure contractors only take projects they are financially capable 
of completing  
C) To restrict out -of-state contractors  
D) To limit the number of projects per year  
VERIFIED ANSWER: B) To ensure contractors only take projects they 
are financially capable of completing  
Rationale: The monetary limit is based on the contractor's net worth and 
financial condition, ensuring they do  not undertake projects beyond their 
financial capacity, which protects the public and subcontractors.  
 
Question 3  
Which Tennessee board issues and regulates the BC -B,C contractor 
license?  
A) Tennessee Real Estate Commission  
B) Tennessee Board for Licensi ng Contractors  
C) Tennessee Department of Labor  
D) Tennessee Safety and Health Board  
VERIFIED

fields.description: NYC Certified IT Administrator (Database) Exam Practice Questions And Correct Verified Answers Plus Rationales 2026 (GUARANTEED PASS)
fields.preview_text:
NYC Certified IT Administrator (Database)  Exam Practice 
Questions And Correct  Verified  Answers Plus Rationales 2026 
(GUARANTEED PASS)  
 
Question 1  
Which SQL command is used to remove all records from a tabl e but keep 
the table structure?  
A) DELETE  
B) TRUNCATE  
C) DROP  
D) REMOVE  
VERIFIED ANSWER: B) TRUNCATE  
Rationale: TRUNCATE removes all rows from a table without affecting its 
structure, unlike DROP which deletes the table entirely, and DELETE which 
can be slower and uses more transaction log space.  
 
Question 2  
What is the default port for MySQL database server?  
A) 1521  
B) 1433  

C) 3306  
D) 5432  
VERIFIED ANSWER: C) 3306  
Rationale: MySQL uses port 3306 by default. Oracle uses 1521, SQL Server 
uses 1433, and PostgreSQL uses 5432.  
 
Question 3  
Which SQL statement is used to m odify existing data in a table?  
A) INSERT  
B) UPDATE  
C) ALTER  
D) MODIFY  
VERIFIED ANSWER: B) UPDATE  
Rationale: UPDATE is used to change existing rows in a table. INSERT adds 
new rows, and ALTER changes table structure.  
 
Question 4  
In relational da tabases, a primary key must be:  
A) Nullable  
B) Duplicate allowed  

C) Foreign key  
D) Unique and not null  
VERIFIED  ANSWER : D) Unique and not null  
Rationale: A primary key uniquely identifies each record in a table and 
cannot contain null values or duplicates.  
 
Question 5  
Which command in SQL is used to permanent ly remove a table and its 
data?  
A) DELETE  
B) UP DATE  
C) DROP TABLE  
D) TRUNCATE  
VERIFIED ANSWER: C) DROP TABLE  
Rationale: DROP TABLE deletes the table structure and all its data 
permanently. TRUNCATE removes data only.  
 
Question 6  
Which of the following is a non -relational database?

Question 1  
What is the primary purpose of a submersib le pump in a water well 
system?  
A) To pressurize the water distribution system  
B) To lift water from the well to th e surface  
C) To filter sediment from the water  
D) To disinfect the water supply  
VERIFIED ANSWER: B) To lift wat er from the well to the surface  
Rationale: Submersible pumps are designed to operate underwater and 
move water efficiently from the well to the  surface without the need for 
priming.  
 
Question 2  
How often should a water well be insp ected for mechanical integrity?  
A) Every 5 years  
B) Every 10 years  

C) At least once a year  
D) Only when problems occur  
VERIFIED ANSWER: C) At least once a year  
Rationale: Annual inspections help ensure that the well and pump system 
operate efficiently and that potential issues are identified early.  
 
Question 3  
What is the primary  function of a pitless adapter?  
A) To increase water pressure  
B) To filter sediment from the water  
C) To provide a sanitary, frost -free connection between the well casing 
and the water line  
D) To measure water temperature  
VERIFIED ANSWER: C) To provide a sanitary, frost -free connection between 
the well casing  and the water line  
Rational e: Pitless adapters allow water to flow from the well below the 
frost line to the distribution system while preventing contamination and 
freezing.  
 
Question 4  

What material is commonly used for the construction of well casings in 
Texas?  
A) Copper and bras s 
B) Steel and PVC  
C) Aluminum and fiberglass  
D) Concrete and clay  
VERIFIED ANSWER: B) Steel and PVC  
Rationale: Steel provides strength and durability for deep wells, while PVC 
offers corrosion resistance and ease of installation for certain 
applications.  
 
Question 5  
What is the main purpose of w ell development after drilling?  
A) To install
